Verse 1

If a man shall steal an a ox, or a sheep, and kill it, or sell it; he shall restore five oxen for an ox, and four sheep for a sheep.

(a) Either a great beast of the herd, or a small beast of the flock.

Verse 2

If a thief be found b breaking up, and be smitten that he die, [there shall] no blood [be shed] for him.

(b) Breaking a house to enter in, or undermining.

Verse 3

If the sun be risen upon him, [there shall be] c blood [shed] for him; [for] he should make full restitution; if he have nothing, then he shall be sold for his theft.

(c) He shall be put to death that kills him.

Verse 8

If the thief be not found, then the master of the house shall be brought unto the judges, [to see] whether he have d put his hand unto his neighbour's goods.

(d) That is, whether he has stolen.

Verse 11

e [Then] shall an oath of the LORD be between them both, that he hath not put his hand unto his neighbour's goods; and the owner of it shall accept [thereof], and he shall not make [it] good.

(e) They should swear by the name of the Lord.

Verse 13

If it be torn in pieces, [then] let him bring f it [for] witness, [and] he shall not make good that which was torn.

(f) He shall show some part of the beast or bring in witnesses.

Verse 15

[But] if the owner thereof [be] with it, he shall not make [it] good: if it [be] an hired [thing], it g came for his hire.

(g) He that hired it shall be free by paying the hire.

Verse 24

And my wrath shall wax hot, and I will kill you with the sword; and your h wives shall be widows, and your children fatherless.

(h) The just plague of God on the oppressors.

Verse 27

For that [is] his covering only, it [is] his raiment for his skin: wherein shall he sleep? and it shall come to pass, when he i crieth unto me, that I will hear; for I [am] gracious.

(i) For cold and necessity.

Verse 29

Thou shalt not delay [to offer] the k first of thy ripe fruits, and of thy liquors: the firstborn of thy sons shalt thou give unto me.

(k) Your abundance of your corn.

Verse 31

And ye shall be holy men unto me: neither shall ye eat [any] flesh [that is] torn of beasts in the field; ye shall cast it l to the dogs.

(l) And so have nothing to do with it.
